tr.th: Conclusion and Recommendations based on this report and previous findings known to us

tr.td: The patient shows elevated creatinine, suggesting impaired renal function, and high urine protein and ketones, indicating possible nephropathy and metabolic stress. Urine is abnormal in appearance and color, with low specific gravity, but no evidence of infection. Blood glucose is normal, but HbA1c is low, possibly due to anemia or recent changes in glycemic control. Recommend further evaluation of renal function and underlying causes for proteinuria and ketonuria.

display.value: Proteinuria due to type 2 diabetes mellitus
Microalbuminuria due to type 2 diabetes mellitus
Acute ST segment elevation myocardial infarction
Disorder of kidney due to diabetes mellitus
